_about: multi-model-team config. Backends (CLIs) and agents are first-class and individually enable/disable-able; routes pick a backend per task (first match wins). Edit this file to add a CLI, flip a backend/agent on or off, or change an agent's role. After editing agents, run scripts/lib/gen_agents.py to regenerate agents/*.md.

defaults._comment: preset biases tier (budget|balanced|premium). fallback is the final hop. quota_fallback is the ordered backend chain tried when the chosen backend is unavailable/exhausted.

backends._comment: Each key is a backend name a route can target. 'enabled' gates use (disabled -> skipped, falls through to the next hop / native). 'kind' selects the invoker in backends.sh (currently only 'gemini' is implemented; 'codex'/'opencode' are config stubs with no invoker yet, so enabling them simply falls through until an invoker is added).

codex._note: Future backend. No invoker implemented yet — enabling it without adding a 'codex' case in backends.sh just falls through to the next hop. Add invocation fields (oneshot_flag, models, etc.) when wired up.

opencode._note: Future backend. Same as codex — config stub until an invoker exists.

agents._comment: Claude Code subagents the model can spawn. 'enabled' controls whether agents/<name>.md is generated (disabled -> the .md is removed so Claude Code won't surface it). 'backend'+'tier' set where the agent relays; 'dispatch' is 'route' (let the router decide) or 'forced' (pin backend+tier). 'role' becomes the agent's description (what the model matches on). Regenerate with scripts/lib/gen_agents.py after editing.

delegate.role: Backend-agnostic dispatcher for STANDARD, VERIFIABLE coding and Gemini's edges where the result is compact or easy to verify: new React/UI components, CSS, SVG/animation, scaffolding, CRUD, REST endpoints, scripts, CLI tools, glue code, SQL, regex, configs, Dockerfiles, unit tests, fixtures, data transforms — plus web-search/doc summarization and bulk text ingestion. Explicitly NOT for reverse-engineering, IL2CPP/protobuf-RE, disassembly, FFI/unsafe, binary, injection/hooking, concurrency, protocol/KCP design, proc-macros, or anything systems-hard (those are Opus-only and never offloaded).

av-research.role: Highest-confidence offload — Gemini's categorical edges that Claude can't do or trails on: watch/summarize video, transcribe/analyze audio, synthesize web research, and summarize large grounded documents. Use when the task is multimodal (A/V/image) or grounded research where a compact, verifiable answer suffices. Not for code logic, RE, or systems work.

bulk-summarizer.role: Cheapest path — pinned to the cheap tier (Gemini Flash). Use for summarize/extract from VERY LARGE text where a short, grounded answer suffices: log dumps, big files, scraped pages, transcripts. Ingestion, not judgment. Not for code logic, RE, or anything where a subtly wrong summary would be costly to catch.

routes._comment: HARD LINE: RE / injection / binary / systems-hard. ALWAYS Opus, checked first so nothing below can offload these.
routes.name: re-injection-heavy

routes._comment: Deep systems / concurrency / architecture — judgment-critical, not commodity.
routes.name: systems-complex

routes._comment: Gemini-exclusive: A/V must go to agy even if it also carries a judgment word, so it is checked before judgment-coding.
routes.name: multimodal

routes._comment: Judgment coding — ordered ABOVE the commodity agy rules so refactor/bugfix/integration go to Sonnet even when they also mention a button/script/config.
routes.name: judgment-coding

routes._comment: bulk-ingest BEFORE grounded-research: both match summarize/extract/ingest/scan; the cheap large-input path must win first or a 30k dump would route standard.
routes.name: bulk-ingest

routes._comment: Standard, verifiable coding — most everyday code work.
routes.name: standard-coding

routes._comment: Default when UNCLASSIFIED — prefer sonnet (safe middle), never agy on a guess.
routes.name: catch-all-safe

proactive._comment: UserPromptSubmit hook. When enabled, a submitted prompt that routes to an agy-kind backend gets a one-shot reminder nudging delegation. Deterministic firing; soft compliance. max_chars/min_chars bound the prompt size (0 = no bound); rules is a CSV allowlist of route names (empty = any agy route).
